`rustc /app/polyglot/main.rs && /app/polyglot/main N`
or
`g++ -x c++ /app/polyglot/main.rs -o /app/polyglot/cmain && /app/polyglot/cmain N`

When I run `rustc /app/polyglot/main.rs`, it will compile and place the binary in the current working directory (because no -o flag is given). If I'm in /app/polyglot/, it will be placed in /app/polyglot/main. But the command says `/app/polyglot/main N` - that would work if the binary is in /app/polyglot/ and named main.
